title: Ensure Image Vulnerability Scanning is enabled

input_row.title: Ensure Image Vulnerability Scanning is enabled
input_row.description: Note: GCR is now deprecated, being superseded by Artifact Registry starting 15th May 2024. Runtime Vulnerability scanning is available via GKE Security Posture Scan images stored in Google Container Registry (GCR) or Artifact Registry (AR) for vulnerabilities.
input_row.rationale: Vulnerabilities in software packages can be exploited by malicious users to obtain unauthorized access to local cloud resources. GCR Container Analysis API or Artifact Registry Container Scanning API allow images stored in GCR or AR respectively to be scanned for known vulnerabilities.
input_row.audit: For Images Hosted in GCR: Using Google Cloud Console: 1. Go to GCR by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/gcr 2. Select Settings and check if Vulnerability scanning is Enabled. Using Command Line: gcloud services list --enabled Ensure that the Container Registry API and Container Analysis API are listed in the output. For Images Hosted in AR: Internal Only - General Using Google Cloud Console: 1. Go to AR by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/artifacts 2. Select Settings and check if Vulnerability scanning is Enabled. Using Command Line: gcloud services list --enabled Ensure that Container Scanning API and Artifact Registry API are listed in the output.
input_row.remediation: For Images Hosted in GCR: Using Google Cloud Console 1. Go to GCR by visiting: https://console.cloud.google.com/gcr 2. Select Settings and, under the Vulnerability Scanning heading, click the TURN ON button. Using Command Line gcloud services enable containeranalysis.googleapis.com For Images Hosted in AR: Using Google Cloud Console 1. Go to GCR by visiting: https://console.cloud.google.com/artifacts 2. Select Settings and, under the Vulnerability Scanning heading, click the ENABLE button. Using Command Line Internal Only - General gcloud services enable containerscanning.googleapis.com
input_row.default_value: By default, GCR Container Analysis and AR Container Scanning are disabled.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The audit process includes specific commands that can be executed via the Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) and the audit field is sufficiently detailed, allowing the verification of whether image vulnerability scanning is enabled for both Google Container Registry (GCR) and Artifact Registry (AR). Additionally, the rationale and remediation details indicate that these properties can be quickly assessed via GCP APIs. Therefore, the control can be verified using automation, adhering to the criteria for an Automated approach.

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_artifact_registry_image_vulnerability_scanning_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- GCP API Calls or Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) methods:
  - Use `gcloud services list --enabled` to verify that the following services are activated:
    - For GCR: `containerregistry.googleapis.com` and `containeranalysis.googleapis.com`
    - For AR: `artifactregistry.googleapis.com` and `containerscanning.googleapis.com`
  
- GCP CLI commands (if applicable):
  - The gcloud command must be executed in the relevant environment where GCR and AR images are stored to check the services.

- Specific resources to enumerate:
  - Container Registry and Artifact Registry resources.

- Exact configuration properties/settings to validate:
  - Check if vulnerability scanning is enabled for images stored in GCR and AR settings.

- Clear Pass/Fail criteria with expected values:
  - **Pass:** Both `containeranalysis.googleapis.com` and `containerregistry.googleapis.com` are listed as enabled for GCR; both `containerscanning.googleapis.com` and `artifactregistry.googleapis.com` are enabled for AR.
  - **Fail:** Any of the required APIs for GCR or AR are not enabled.  

This structured approach allows for a comprehensive automation strategy to ensure compliance with the control regarding image vulnerability scanning.
